mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 58% during documented recovery phases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force equity repricing downward within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of industrial production contraction months during tightening periods, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicate inflationary pressure that outpaces monetary accommodation, compressing real yields and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses sign, producing positive 20-day returns when both inflation and rates rise, or the combined signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue projections, mechanically lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with payroll growth (Spearman rho < 0.05) or the signal predicts negative returns in over 55% of expansionary months.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers immediate recession fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of quarters where real GDP contracts by over 0.5%, or the signal fails to separate recessionary from expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth that outpaces unemployment rate declines during a post-shock recovery phase signals robust capacity utilization that supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails to predict positive 20-day returns when industrial production rises alongside falling unemployment, or the interaction term becomes insignificant during recovery periods.
